problem: Before committing to a model for a large classification pipeline, we needed to compare accuracy, latency (p50/p95), throughput, and cost across multiple Claude and open-weight models on our actual data.
solution: Used /llm-benchmark to run all candidate models in parallel as background processes, aggregate JSON result files into a structured comparison table, and get a cost/performance recommendation — with error rates flagged automatically.
author: CEPS AI Team

problem: Long-running classification and embedding jobs (6–12 hours) on GPU VMs get killed when the SSH session disconnects, and there's no standard way to monitor progress or recover from failures.
solution: Used /vm-job to launch jobs in a tmux session with nohup fallback, write a status file alongside the log, and get a clean monitor/re-attach command. Job survives disconnects and progress is checkable anytime.
author: CEPS AI Team

problem: Technical implementation plans tend to anchor on the first reasonable scope rather than the best possible product. It's hard to know when a plan is thinking too small before committing to it.
solution: Used /gstack plan-ceo-review to challenge premises, find the 10-star version of the product, and choose from four review modes — scope expansion, selective expansion, hold scope, or scope reduction — before any code was written.
author: CEPS AI Team

problem: After running LLM classification on 427,000 ODA project descriptions, the team needed a structured way to audit a sample — checking binary labels, 3-way tech categories, and borderline reclassifications — without any server infrastructure.
solution: Used /result-evaluator to generate a self-contained HTML review tool with sampled records embedded. Ratings and notes persist in localStorage, keyboard shortcuts speed up review, and results export to CSV — zero setup required.
author: CEPS AI Team

problem: A 156,000-item batch classification job runs for hours across chunks. Without a status check, it's impossible to know how far it has progressed, which chunks failed, or when it will finish.
solution: Used /classify-status to scan all output chunk files, count completed vs remaining items, surface failed or empty chunks, and get a clean summary with a suggested recovery command — without interrupting the running job.
author: CEPS AI Team
